channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Wizards of the Coast Is Getting in on Its Own ‘Dungeons & Dragons’ Actual Play
item.description: 'Dungeon Masters' will feature both current and unreleased material from the legendary TTRPG in its first in-house actual play series.

item.title: Iceland Just Got its First Mosquitoes. Scientists Aren’t Ready for What Comes Next
item.description: As the Arctic's climate and ecology rapidly change, two researchers are calling for a paradigm shift in insect monitoring.

item.title: The Battle for OpenAI’s Soul
item.description: In Musk v. Altman, a jury will soon determine whether OpenAI has strayed from its founding mission to ensure AGI benefits humanity. Here’s what to know.

item.title: What is the release date for Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 8 on CBS and Paramount+?
item.description: Judge Ayers has come out of nowhere to deliver heaps of drama. But when does Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 8 hit CBS and Paramount+?

item.title: Europe Could Run Out of Jet Fuel in Just 6 Weeks
item.description: The head of the International Energy Agency says it will take "up to two years to come back where we were before the war.”

item.title: Many are still leaving the door open': Security experts warn FIFA World Cup partners could be putting customers at risk of email attacks
item.description: Some World Cup partners are not actively protecting their domain name with the strongest DMARC policy.

item.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 to Remind Everyone How Great Mythos Is
item.description: Bold strategy to promote your new release as "less broadly capable" than other options.

item.title: The UK Launches Its $675 Million Sovereign AI Fund
item.description: In a bid to minimize dependence on technology from other countries, the UK government is plowing resources into homegrown AI startups.

item.title: Samsung TV OLED, RTX 5070 et Garmin Forerunner 970 : les 3 meilleures promos high-tech du jeudi 16 avril 2026
item.description: Ce jeudi 16 avril 2026, les bons plans high-tech sont particulièrement généreux avec de grosses remises : découvrez les offres qui valent le détour.

item.title: Why fans are so upset about Patrick Schwarzeneggers Beach Read casting
item.description: Patrick Schwarzenegger's casting as Gus Everett in the "Beach Read" adaptation has sparked backlash online, with fans arguing he does not match the Emily Henry character.

item.title: The Next Surface PCs May Not Be the Big Upgrades You Hoped for
item.description: Expect Microsoft's next Surface Pro and Surface Laptops to cost more for a simple refresh.

item.title: Metro’s unique flavor of post-apocalyptic misery is back in Metro 2039 — and I can’t wait to return to the ‘dark heart of the Moscow metro’ where ‘hope is lost' and 'the future looks bleak, if there is one’
item.description: Metro’s particular style of post-apocalyptic bleakness and misery is back with Metro 2039, and I’ve already reinstalled Metro Exodus to get in the mood.

item.title: A first look at Metro 2039 shows how its Ukrainian developer turned the darkness up to 11
item.description: If the real world isn’t grim enough for you, Ukranian developer 4A Games has your back: Metro 2039 has been announced and is scheduled to arrive this winter. And based on the developer’s first look at the title, Metro 2039 looks to be an even darker affair than previous titles in the series. A tall order, but the real-world turmoil that has enveloped 4A Games since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine sounds like it has turned into a painful inspiration for the developer.The lengthy cinematic reveal, which also contains a brief bit of gameplay at the end, doesn’t give much of the story away. But it do...

item.title: It’s not just you — Bluesky is (sorta) down
item.description: Bluesky has been experiencing ongoing service disruptions since just before 3 a.m. ET.

item.title: Allbirds goes soleless and pivots to AI
item.description: Allbirds was a brand that made wool shoes that were popular with Silicon Valley tech dudes. Emphasis on "was."

item.title: The nine best ways to protect, customize, and accessorize your MacBook Neo
item.description: The MacBook Neo is poised to be a top-selling laptop in 2026, thanks in large part to its affordable $599 entry price. Despite launching at a more accessible price point than Apple’s $1,099 (though often discounted) 2026 MacBook Air, the Neo makes surprisingly few concessions in terms of build quality. Its design has the same […]

item.title: Everything we like is a psyop
item.description: If culture is shaped by TikTok's algorithmic feed, then it's time for us to decide where we draw the line between necessary marketing and inauthentic growth hacking.

item.title: Why AI is the ultimate accelerator for creativity
item.description: The technology has the capacity to free up human capacity for the work that only we can do: imagining, connecting, and creating meaning. Here’s how to use it to that end Have you noticed that in the current discourse around artificial intelligence, the narrative often slips into one of two extremes? There is either a techno-utopian dream of total automation or a dystopian nightmare where human agency is erased. But there are other options! As we navigate this inflection point in civilization, I invite you to consider a third path: pragmatic optimism....

item.title: Eureka Ergonomic Opal standing desk review: Beautiful, stylish, and solid — but I can't ignore the mediocre storage
item.description: The Eureka Ergonomic Opal is a premium design standing desk with solid construction, less-than-satisfactory cable management and cramped drawers, yet a beautiful design.

item.title: ‘Lee Cronin’s The Mummy’ Should Be Called ‘Lee Cronin’s The Exorcist’
item.description: The new creature feature unwraps plenty of gore but too much déjà vu.

item.title: Google's AI Mode Update Tries to Kill Tab Hopping in Chrome
item.description: Google latest update to AI Mode in its Chrome browser is designed to keep the chatbot-style search tool always around once you start an online search journey.

item.title: OpenAI’s big Codex update is a direct shot at Anthropic’s Claude Code
item.description: OpenAI is beefing up its agentic coding and development system Codex with a suite of updates that let it use your computer, generate images, and remember from past experiences. Codex will now be able to operate desktop apps on your computer, OpenAI says in a blog post announcing the update. It can work in the […]

item.title: Google’s AI Mode update lets you open links without leaving the page
item.description: Google is upgrading AI Mode in Chrome with a new feature that will allow you to open links to sources alongside your chat. Now, instead of automatically opening a new tab, clicking a source will open the website side by side with AI Mode, allowing you to ask follow-up questions about what's on the page. […]

item.title: Google now lets you explore the web side-by-side with AI Mode
item.description: Now, when you're using AI Mode on Chrome desktop, clicking a link will open the web page side-by-side with AI Mode.

item.title: 'I would've loved to have directed that one': Exit 8's Genki Kawamura on why he's excited for A24's Backrooms horror movie
item.description: Genki Kawamura is a fan of the Backrooms and admitted he would've loved to adapt that story for the screen.

item.title: IPVanish has begun offering Amazon gift cards when you refer a friend — here's how much you could earn
item.description: IPVanish has officially launched its first-ever refer-a-friend program. Current users can earn Amazon or Home Depot gift cards, while new subscribers score a sweet 10% discount on their plan.

item.title: OpenAI's latest Codex update builds the groundwork for its upcoming super app
item.description: Last month, following reporting from The Wall Street Journal, OpenAI confirmed it was working on a desktop super app that would combine ChatGPT, its Codex coding agent and Atlas web browser into one cohesive experience. OpenAI is not releasing that application today. Instead, it's pushing out a major update to Codex that significantly expands what that software can do. However, the new release offers a glimpse of what OpenAI hopes to build with its latest effort. "We're building the super app out in the open," said Thibault Sottiaux, the head of Codex, during a press briefing held by OpenAI. ...

item.title: Google Chrome makes it easier to wrangle different tabs in AI Mode
item.description: Love 'em or hate 'em, no modern browser is complete without robust tab support, and so too would it seem Google's AI Mode. Starting today, the company is rolling out an update to users in the US that makes the tool better at interacting and understanding tabs. To start, the next time you use AI Mode on Chrome for desktop and click on a link, the chatbot will open a new side-by-side interface that allows you to both browse the new webpage and ask questions of AI Mode. The connection allows the chatbot to maintain the context of the search that brought you to that website in the first place. For...

item.title: Intel launches new Core Series 3 chips for mainstream laptops
item.description: Intel has unveiled its new Core Series 3 chips, the official title for its Wildcat Lake-codenamed series intended for mainstream and value-oriented laptops. Built using the same Intel 18A process as its Core Ultra Series 3 chips, they’re significantly more powerful than the previous generation and promise "exceptional battery life" and "boosted AI-ready performance." Intel says the Core Series 3 offers up to 47 percent better single-thread performance and 41 percent better multi-thread performance, as well as 2.8x better GPU AI performance compared to a five-year-old PC. Stacked up against its...

item.title: The Newest Alzheimer’s Drugs Might Be Worthless, Review Finds
item.description: Anti-amyloid drugs likely provide no "clinically meaningful" effects for people with Alzheimer's, the authors concluded.

item.title: DJI has teased a new entry-level drone — but it'll have to be special to outshine my beloved Neo 2
item.description: DJI seems poised to launch two new entry-level camera drones — a move that seems a little strange given it already offers several cheap, affordable and highly capable models.

item.title: How to watch MasterChef Australia online – stream Meghan Markle on Masterchef from anywhere
item.description: As Meghan Markle debuts as a judge on Masterchef Australia 2026 (season 18)here's how to watch MasterChef Australia online and from anywhere around the world with a the right VPN.

item.title: Moscow chokes international internet bandwidth in latest attack on Russian VPN users
item.description: Russia’s fight against digital privacy escalates as internet providers agree to freeze the expansion of cross-border channels into Europe. By capping international bandwidth, authorities aim to force telecom operators to either filter VPN traffic or hike prices, making it harder for citizens to bypass state censorship.

item.title: Google just dropped a 50% discount on a year of YouTube Premium with Google One Premium — here’s how you can check if you’re eligible
item.description: This half-price YouTube Premium discount with Google One Premium is not to be skipped over.

item.title: This sleek new espresso machine from Philips promises 'that unmistakable cafe-like taste' at home — and I can't wait to try it
item.description: The Philips Café Aromis can use larger doses of coffee, and has a 'conversational' assistant that adjusts brew settings automatically to suit your preferences.

item.title: Le forfait idéal pour les ponts de mai vient de faire son apparition sous la barre des 16 euros
item.description: RED by SFR met en avant un abonnement très complet à 15,99 euros par mois, pensé pour ceux qui voyagent régulièrement hors des frontières européennes.

item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream devs say the Mii programming was 'pure chaos and 'really hard to manage' during development, that it took 'six or seven years' to fine-tune
item.description: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream lead programmer Takaomi Ueno has said one of the most challenging aspects of development was Mii interactions and that it took around "six or seven years" to get right.

item.title: SpaceX and Other Elon Musk Companies Are Propping Up Cybertruck Sales
item.description: SpaceX alone reportedly accounted for nearly 20% of Cybertrucks registered in the U.S. in late 2025.

item.title: Bolides : quelque chose d’inhabituel s’est produit dans le ciel en mars, et personne n’a encore d’explication
item.description: Au cours du mois de mars 2026, les signalements de boules de feu observées dans le ciel ont explosé partout dans le monde. Simple effet de saison, hausse réelle des gros bolides ou phénomène encore mal compris ? Derrière les témoignages spectaculaires, une question demeure : que s’est-il...

item.title: Secretlab Magnus Evo standing desk review: Streamlined and refined but unmistakably Secretlab in spirit
item.description: The Secretlab Magnus Evo is a refined and simplified standing desk with a few nice features and none of the excess.

item.title: « Des prix excessifs » : Ticketmaster et Live Nation reconnus coupables de surfacturation
item.description: Live Nation et Ticketmaster ont été reconnus coupables de monopole et de surfacturation des billets par un jury fédéral à New York.

item.title: Two Americans sentenced for helping North Korea steal $5 million in fake IT worker scheme
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced that two Americans were sentenced to years in prison for helping the North Korean government place fake IT workers in U.S. companies.

item.title: Webb Telescope Resolves Cosmic Identity Crisis Between Planets and Stars
item.description: Where do massive planets end and stars begin? It may come down to how they formed.

item.title: Microsoft planning Surface Laptop with an OLED display
item.description: Microsoft is preparing to launch new Surface Pro and Surface Laptop models in the coming months. Windows Central reports that the refreshed models will include both Intel and Qualcomm variants, with Microsoft opting for an OLED display option for the Surface Laptop this year. Microsoft is expected to launch new Surface Pro and Laptop models […]

item.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
item.description: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong — it's also diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is part of it.

item.title: AI traffic to US retailers rose 393% in Q1, and it’s boosting their revenue too
item.description: Adobe says AI traffic to U.S. retail sites also jumped 269% in March, with visitors converting better and generating more revenue than non-AI shoppers.
